skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Guides collaborative discovery before creative or implementation work by clarifying goals, constraints, trade-offs, and design details.
skill.summary: Turn early ideas into validated designs before implementation begins.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by ordinary Markdown prose and headings in SKILL.md. The backtick execution alert at line 38 is also false positive because it marks a documentation path. The skill does instruct file creation and a git commit.

low_findings.title: Workspace and Git Modification Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to write a validated design document and commit it to git. This is legitimate planning behavior, but it can modify the workspace and repository history, so users should review the document before committing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly describe writing a design document and committing it to git. The behavior is clear, but it is not malicious because it is tied to the stated documentation workflow.

low_findings.title: Static Dangerous Pattern Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to normal prose and Markdown headings, not cryptographic algorithms or secret handling. The backtick execution alert at line 38 is a Markdown-formatted path, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain Markdown instructions only, with no crypto API usage, key material, hashing calls, shell interpolation, or executable script content.

content.value_statement: Early product and engineering ideas often lack clear goals, constraints, and success criteria. This skill guides a structured dialogue that turns rough ideas into reviewed design plans.
actual_capabilities: Reviews the current project context before proposing a design.
Asks one focused question at a time to clarify intent and constraints.
Compares two or three approaches with trade-offs and a recommendation.
Presents design sections in manageable 200 to 300 word chunks.
Covers architecture, components, data flow, errors, and testing.
Can document the validated design in a dated plans file.
limitations: It does not implement the final design by itself.
It depends on user answers for unclear requirements.
It may ask several rounds of questions before producing a design.
It can suggest git commits, which users should approve before execution.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Clarify goals, user flows, constraints, and implementation options before writing code.

use_cases.title: Convert Ideas Into Specs
use_cases.description: Turn a loose product concept into a structured design that stakeholders can review.

use_cases.title: Prepare Refactoring Work
use_cases.description: Explore trade-offs and risks before changing existing architecture or behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a Rough Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me brainstorm this idea before implementation: [describe the idea]. Ask one question at a time until the goal is clear.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Design Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Explore two or three approaches for [feature or change]. Include trade-offs, risks, and your recommended option.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Reviewable Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn our clarified requirements into a design plan. Cover architecture, components, data flow, error handling, and testing.

prompt_templates.title: Document a Validated Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: After we validate the design, draft a concise plan document for docs/plans with decisions, open questions, and next steps.

output_examples.input: I want to add team invitations to our app.
output_examples.output: The skill asks who can invite users, what roles exist, how invites expire, and how success will be measured before proposing designs.

output_examples.input: We need to redesign onboarding but I am not sure where to start.
output_examples.output: The skill breaks the problem into goals, user segments, constraints, data needs, and a small set of implementation approaches.

output_examples.input: Help me plan a risky refactor.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies affected components, migration options, rollback needs, testing strategy, and review checkpoints.

best_practices: Start with the business or user problem, not the preferred implementation.
Answer each question with concrete constraints and success criteria.
Review each design section before moving to implementation planning.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to skip discovery and produce a final plan immediately.
Do not treat the recommended approach as approved without review.
Do not allow automatic commits without checking the generated document.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on clarifying ideas and producing a design before implementation.

faq.question: Can it work with an existing repository?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to inspect project context before asking design questions.

faq.question: Why does it ask only one question at a time?
faq.answer: Single questions keep the discussion focused and reduce confusion during discovery.

faq.question: Can it create a design document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft a validated design in a dated file under docs/plans.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but users should review file writes and git commits.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
